No Poverty.reason: The course includes "Liberation Theologies" and "Minjung Liberation," which address the plight of the poor and oppressed, aligning with pro-poor strategies (Target 1.b).
evidence: Minjung Liberation and Asian Pentecostal Theology

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to ending hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly discusses "Divine Healing" as part of Pentecostal theology, providing a tangential but direct link to the concept of health and well-being.
evidence: Divine Healing: Sacramental signs of salvation

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3). It also explicitly aims to broaden understanding of diverse religious traditions and foster dialogue, aligning with Target 4.7.
